ctxMarket: Attention is focused on Solana’s security posture following a major exploit, as well as diverging signals between price action and fundamentals. There is also discussion around the growing adoption of real-world assets (RWA) on Solana and how it compares to Ethereum.
ctxTalking:
• The $285M Drift Protocol hack, attributed to North Korean actors after months of infiltration
• Solana’s transaction activity hitting January lows, raising questions about price direction versus fundamentals
• Rapid growth in Solana RWA holders and its competition with Ethereum’s tokenized gold
ctxMatters:
Security incidents like the Drift hack highlight ongoing risks for DeFi users and protocols on Solana.  
Divergence between Solana’s fundamentals and price action is fueling debate about the network’s outlook.  
Rising RWA adoption signals expanding use cases but also intensifies competition with Ethereum.
ctxSignal:
Signal: The Drift exploit’s sophisticated social engineering and the launch of new Solana security initiatives (STRIDE, Incident Response Network).
Noise: Speculation about immediate price crashes without clear fundamental triggers.
ctxGlossary:
RWA — Real-world assets, such as tokenized gold, represented on blockchain platforms  
Drift Protocol — A Solana-based DeFi trading platform recently targeted in a major exploit

readMore.title: Altcoin Resilience Signals 'Compelling Entry Points' for Crypto Markets
Solana transactions hit January 2026 lows – Will SOL repeat its 32% crash?
Raising the Bar on Solana Ecosystem Security
